text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared central repository. Instead of waiting until the end of a project, developers integrate their work several times a day. An automated build system is triggered each time new code is pushed, which compiles the project and then runs tests. This process happens in a CI pipeline, which is a set of steps that prepare the code for release. By running these tests immediately, CI helps catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. This saves time and effort, ensuring the code remains stable and reliable throughout development.
